---
package: "openpipeline"
version: "v4.2.0"
title: "dimensionality_reduction"
namespace: "Workflows/multiomics"
description: "Run calculations that output information required for most integration methods: PCA, nearest neighbour and UMAP."
type: "workflow"
toc: false
latest: "true"
subtitle: "openpipeline v4.2.0"
---

::: {.column-margin}

### Info

ID: `dimensionality_reduction`  
Namespace: `workflows/multiomics`

### Links

[{{< fa brands github >}} View source code](https://github.com/openpipelines-bio/openpipeline/tree/v4.2.0/src/workflows/multiomics/dimensionality_reduction)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Browse all versions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Get run instructions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline/v4.2.0/components/workflows/multiomics/dimensionality_reduction)

:::
## Visualisation

```{mermaid}
flowchart TB
    c_dimred_pca["pca"]
    c_workflows_multiomics_neighbors_leiden_umap["neighbors_leiden_umap"]
    c_dimred_pca --> c_workflows_multiomics_neighbors_leiden_umap
    style c_dimred_pca fill:#e2f4e7,stroke:#2b8f52,color:#13201a
    style c_workflows_multiomics_neighbors_leiden_umap fill:#e2f4e7,stroke:#2b8f52,color:#13201a
```


## Argument groups

### Inputs

|Name         |Description                                                                           |Attributes                                  |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--id`       |ID of the sample.                                                                     |`string`, required, example: `"foo"`        |
|`--input`    |Path to the sample.                                                                   |`file`, required, example: `"dataset.h5mu"` |
|`--layer`    |use specified layer for expression values instead of the .X object from the modality. |`string`, default: `"log_normalized"`       |
|`--modality` |Which modality to process.                                                            |`string`, default: `"rna"`                  |


### Outputs

|Name       |Description                     |Attributes                                 |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--output` |Destination path to the output. |`file`, required, example: `"output.h5mu"` |


### PCA options

|Name                          |Description                                                                                                                                                  |Attributes                   |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--obsm_pca`                  |In which .obsm slot to store the resulting PCA embedding.                                                                                                    |`string`, default: `"X_pca"` |
|`--var_pca_feature_selection` |Column name in .var matrix that will be used to select which genes to run the PCA on.                                                                        |`string`                     |
|`--pca_loadings_varm_output`  |Name of the .varm key where the PCA loadings are stored.                                                                                                     |`string`                     |
|`--pca_variance_uns_output`   |Name of the .uns key where the variance and variance ratio will be stored as a map. The map will contain two keys: variance and variance_ratio respectively. |`string`                     |
|`--pca_overwrite`             |Allow overwriting slots for PCA output.                                                                                                                      |`boolean_true`               |


### Neighbour calculation

|Name                             |Description                                                                             |Attributes                            |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--uns_neighbors`                |In which .uns slot to store various neighbor output objects.                            |`string`, default: `"neighbors"`      |
|`--obsp_neighbor_distances`      |In which .obsp slot to store the distance matrix between the resulting neighbors.       |`string`, default: `"distances"`      |
|`--obsp_neighbor_connectivities` |In which .obsp slot to store the connectivities matrix between the resulting neighbors. |`string`, default: `"connectivities"` |


### Umap options

|Name          |Description                                                |Attributes                    |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--obsm_umap` |In which .obsm slot to store the resulting UMAP embedding. |`string`, default: `"X_umap"` |

## Authors


  * Dries Schaumont [{{< fa brands github >}}](https://github.com/DriesSchaumont) [{{< fa brands orcid >}}](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-4389-0440) [{{< fa brands linkedin >}}](https://linkedin.com/in/dries-schaumont) (author)

## Example commands

You can run the pipeline using `nextflow run`.

### View help

You can use `--help` as a parameter to get an overview of the possible parameters.

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline \
  -r v4.2.0 -latest \
  -main-script target/nextflow/workflows/multiomics/dimensionality_reduction/main.nf \
  --help
```

### Run command

<details>
<summary>Example of `params.yaml`</summary>

Generate a `params.yaml` for this component on [Viash Hub](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline/v4.2.0/components/workflows/multiomics/dimensionality_reduction).

</details>

Run locally with:

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline \
  -r v4.2.0 -latest \
  -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/workflows/multiomics/dimensionality_reduction/main.nf \
  -params-file params.yaml
```

:::{.callout-note}
Replace `-profile docker` with `-profile podman` or `-profile singularity` depending on the desired backend.
:::

Running on Seqera or through Viash Hub is explained in the [guides](/guides/index.qmd).
